artifacts.errorReason: Official UGent Research Tips URL returned a 404 page and was not used as evidence.

artifacts.claimText: Ghent University has a university-wide framework for responsible AI use that guides the Ghent University community with principles including accountability, honesty, respectful use, careful and sustainable use, privacy and confidentiality, and exemplary behaviour.

citation.citationTitle: University-wide framework for responsible use of generative AI at Ghent University
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Ghent University says everyone at the university has access to Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat with business data protection, while also noting that Copilot Chat is not promoted as the sole GenAI tool.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: University-wide framework for responsible use of generative AI at Ghent University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: These values serve as a guide for the entire Ghent University community and all activities that constitute university life. Responsible use encompasses the following fundamental principles: (1) accountability and independent action, (2) honesty, (3) accessibility and respectful use, (4) careful and sustainable use, (5) privacy and confidentiality, and (6) exemplary behaviour.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: From the 2024-2025 academic year, the following guidelines will apply to assignments you complete at home: responsible use of generative AI tools is permitted. Please note: an individual teacher in a specific subject can still prohibit its use.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI in Education at Ghent University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: There are some clear cases where you have used GenAI irresponsibly and thus committed an irregularity: Plagiarism is plagiarism, whether or not you used GenAI tools. You let GenAI generate fake data and use it as real data. You outsource the thinking process to GenAI. The above irregularities lead to an exam disciplinary procedure.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: When does the use of GenAI count as fraud? section

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative AI in Ghent University Education: Impact and Approach
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Responsible GenAI use is always permitted unless a lecturer explicitly prohibits its use for parts of an assignment, requiring that assignment to take place in a controlled environment. For homework assignments, such a ban is neither possible nor desirable to enforce. The current detection tools are unreliable.

artifacts.sourceTitle: GenAI: Translating Research Integrity into Responsible (Gen)AI Use
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: As a member of Ghent University, you are expected to demonstrate research integrity. (Gen)AI may not be used to infringe upon or to justify any violation of research integrity. Researchers are transparant about substantial use of (gen)AI, according to the standards of their discipline.

artifacts.sourceTitle: GDPR: What should I take into account when developing or using AI?
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: When you develop artificial intelligence based on personal data or process personal data using artificial intelligence in your research, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) applies. AI applications must therefore ensure privacy and data protection principles throughout their lifecycle, including the principles of privacy by design and privacy by default.

artifacts.title: Ghent University AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Staged crawl for Ghent University verified central official GenAI guidance, the university-wide responsible GenAI framework, Copilot/tool guidance, student guidance, teaching guidance, and research/privacy guidance. Claims are limited to responsible-use principles, Copilot availability and data-protection positioning, student assignment use rules, academic integrity consequences, teaching assessment guidance, privacy-sensitive data handling, research-integrity expectations, and GDPR treatment for AI using personal data.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This run did not access SharePoint intranet or Ufora pages and does not infer policy from login-gated materials.
Faculty-scoped Ghent University sources were rejected for university-wide claims where central sources were available; they may contain additional local rules not represented here.
Candidate claims are staged artifacts only and are not canonical public claims until separately reviewed and promoted.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
